title: SSTO Spaceplane Feasibility Problem Statement
description: Define the problem context and assessment objective for a reusable single-stage-to-orbit spaceplane. The assessment addresses the orbital SSTO mission only and does not treat hypersonic point-to-point transport as an equivalent mission class. The vehicle is a reusable integrated airframe-propulsion orbital vehicle with horizontal take-off and landing and cryogenic LH2/LOX propellant architecture.
notes: Orbital SSTO only; runway-based operations; combined-cycle or rocket propulsion; fully reusable primary structure and TPS.

title: System-of-Systems Closure Problem Definition
description: Define the core engineering closure problem: can a reusable SSTO spaceplane achieve orbital insertion and runway recovery in a single stage while preserving sufficient payload and acceptable operability and safety? Feasibility requires simultaneous closure across propulsion, mass fraction, thermal survivability, GNC, trajectory, operability, and regulatory domains.
notes: Failure to close any single discipline can invalidate the full concept. Seven coupled closure dimensions.

title: Governing Technical Challenges Identification
description: Identify and characterise the five governing technical challenge areas: propulsion across the full Mach-altitude envelope, structural and mass fraction closure, aerothermodynamic survivability, stability guidance and control, and trajectory and mission closure.
notes: Propulsion Mach 0 to orbital; dry-mass fraction < 10-12%; reusable TPS; full-envelope GNC; trajectory closure.

title: Operational and Regulatory Challenge Framing
description: Frame the operational challenges including turnaround, TPS inspection, cryogenic servicing, and dispatch reliability; and the regulatory challenges arising from dual-domain governance across airworthiness and launch licensing.
notes: Turnaround aspiration <= 48 hours; hybrid compliance basis across aviation and launch domains.

title: Assessment Scope and Reference Targets
description: Define the scope of assessment across nine areas and establish reference targets for mission, reusability, infrastructure, economics, and safety. Reference payload: 5-10 metric tons to approximately 200 km circular LEO.
notes: 9 assessment areas; reference cost target < $10M/mission as stress-test; safety targets decomposed by category.

title: Primary Uncertainties and Pacing Technologies
description: Identify and track dominant uncertainties governing concept viability: propulsion mode transition, mass fraction closure, reusable TPS closure, cryogenic structural integration, abort and safe-return coverage, regulatory basis, and economic competitiveness.
notes: Seven primary uncertainty filters; these are principal filters through which the concept must pass.

title: Historical Context and Competitive Benchmark
description: Frame the concept against historical SSTO programmes (NASP/X-30, VentureStar, Skylon) and current competitive benchmarks from partially reusable multi-stage systems such as Falcon and Starship.
notes: Historical programmes limited by coupled closure problems; must demonstrate advantage over improving reusable multi-stage.

title: Mission Needs Definition
description: Define and formalise the mission needs that justify assessment of a reusable SSTO spaceplane. Mission needs represent the operational gaps and strategic drivers (the 'why') and do not constitute fixed system requirements. Twelve mission need statements (MN-01 through MN-12) cover direct reusable orbital access, runway-based operability, full-envelope integration, operationally meaningful reuse, reduced mission complexity, controlled return, viable infrastructure, economic defensibility, certifiable governance, pacing-technology identification, abort and safe-return coverage, and TSTO fallback boundary.
notes: Mission needs are the capability rationale; they support later derivation of requirements, KPPs, MOEs, and go/no-go criteria.

title: Governing Uncertainties Register and Closure Evidence
description: Define and register the governing uncertainties (GU-01 through GU-14 primary, GU-B1 through GU-B3 SSTO-specific) whose non-closure would invalidate the SSTO architecture or mandate redirection to TSTO. Each uncertainty includes governance rationale and required closure evidence. Covers propulsion-sequence closure, forebody-inlet-engine operability, mode-transition controllability, dry-mass fraction and volume closure, cryogenic integration, TPS and active cooling, thermal-structural life, GNC across regimes, trajectory closure, abort coverage, ground operability, regulatory basis, and economic competitiveness.
notes: 17 governing uncertainties total; their resolution determines concept viability or redirection to TSTO.
